findings.title: SSO/SAML Integration Dependencies
findings.description: Implementing SSO/SAML integration requires dependencies on third-party identity providers (Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin) and must support both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated flows. This integration is critical for enterprise onboarding.
assumptions: Third-party identity providers will provide necessary documentation and support for integration.

evidence.excerpt: Need to integrate with Okta, Azure AD, and OneLogin.

recommendations: Engage with identity providers early to understand integration requirements.

findings.title: Data Isolation Compliance Risk
findings.description: The current application-level row filtering for tenant data isolation poses a compliance risk, especially with SOC 2 Type II requirements. A proper isolation mechanism needs to be implemented to mitigate this risk.
assumptions: The security audit findings are accurate and reflect the current state of the application.

evidence.excerpt: Security audit flagged that tenant data isolation relies on application-level row filtering.

recommendations: Evaluate and implement database-level isolation mechanisms.

findings.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
findings.description: To meet enterprise customer needs, a robust RBAC system must be developed that includes custom role definitions and resource-level access controls. This is essential for compliance and security.
assumptions: Existing user management systems can be adapted to support RBAC.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers need fine-grained RBAC beyond our current admin/member/viewer model.

recommendations: Design and implement a new RBAC framework.

findings.title: Data Residency Controls Implementation
findings.description: Implementing data residency controls requires changes to the database architecture to support region-pinned storage. This is necessary for compliance with GDPR and data sovereignty requirements.

evidence.excerpt: Need to implement region-pinned storage with cross-region replication disabled by default.

recommendations: Assess database capabilities and implement necessary changes.

findings.title: White-Label Theming and Custom Domain Support
findings.description: To meet enterprise branding requirements, the platform needs to support white-label theming and custom domains. This will enhance customer satisfaction and marketability.
assumptions: Existing architecture can be adapted to support multiple domains.

evidence.excerpt: Three enterprise prospects require white-label branding with custom logos, color schemes, and domain names.

recommendations: Evaluate current architecture for adaptability to white-labeling.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with implementing SSO/SAML integration, data isolation compliance, and role-based access control. The phased delivery plan suggests prioritizing SSO and data isolation for MVP, with RBAC and data residency controls in V1, and white-label theming in V2. All findings are based on validated evidence from the original tickets.
